summary: 美国与伊朗达成初步谅解备忘录，特朗普称其为'无条件投降'，但伊朗仍控制霍尔木兹海峡并设定谈判条件。原定和平谈判因以色列与真主党冲突取消，副总统万斯批评以色列极右翼官员，引发外交争议。共和党参议员普遍担忧协议中3000亿美元重建基金实为变相赎金。华盛顿反思池新漆剥落，引发环境问题讨论。

vocabulary.en: surrender without any conditions or demands
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump Claims Iran Agreement is 'Unconditional Surrender'
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普声称伊朗协议是'无条件投降'

vocabulary.en: a document describing a preliminary agreement between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: the MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender
vocabulary.example_cn: 这份谅解备忘录看起来不像无条件投降

vocabulary.en: the blocking of a port or coastline by naval forces to prevent trade or military movement
vocabulary.excerpt: the naval blockade has been lifted
vocabulary.example_cn: 海上封锁已经解除

vocabulary.cn: 霍尔木兹海峡（连接波斯湾与印度洋的战略水道）
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula, crucial for global oil transport
vocabulary.excerpt: The Strait of Hormuz is technically open
vocabulary.example_cn: 霍尔木兹海峡理论上已开放

vocabulary.en: minor difficulties or obstacles in a process
vocabulary.excerpt: I think we had to expect a lot of bumps in the road
vocabulary.example_cn: 我认为我们必须预料到很多挫折

vocabulary.en: not clearly expressed or defined
vocabulary.excerpt: this is a very vague and very partial agreement
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一份非常模糊且不完整的协议

vocabulary.cn: 部分协议；不完整的协议
vocabulary.en: an agreement that covers only some aspects of a dispute
vocabulary.excerpt: this is a very vague and very partial agreement
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一份非常模糊且不完整的协议

vocabulary.en: to impose a legal or moral obligation
vocabulary.excerpt: it did not bind Israel to anything
vocabulary.example_cn: 它没有对以色列施加任何约束

vocabulary.cn: 真主党（黎巴嫩什叶派政治和军事组织）
vocabulary.en: a Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and militant group
vocabulary.excerpt: It did not bind Hezbollah to anything
vocabulary.example_cn: 它没有对真主党施加任何约束

vocabulary.en: an advantage or option that can be used in a situation
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran now has a card to play, which is peace in Lebanon
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗现在有了一张可打的牌，那就是黎巴嫩的和平

vocabulary.en: an attack by military aircraft
vocabulary.excerpt: 16 people were killed after Israeli airstrikes
vocabulary.example_cn: 以色列空袭造成16人死亡

vocabulary.en: to cause disagreement or conflict between groups
vocabulary.excerpt: it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗的目标是在美国和以色列之间制造分裂

vocabulary.en: to discourage someone from doing something through fear of consequences
vocabulary.excerpt: that rests on deterring what are very real threats to your people
vocabulary.example_cn: 这依赖于威慑那些对人民构成的真实威胁

vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms of a deal
vocabulary.excerpt: because there is now this memorandum of understanding with Iran
vocabulary.example_cn: 因为现在有了这份与伊朗的谅解备忘录

vocabulary.cn: 神权疯子（贬义，指伊朗宗教领袖）

vocabulary.excerpt: giving billions of dollars to theocratic lunatics who want to murder us

vocabulary.example_cn: 这是整件事中最令人费解的部分

vocabulary.example_cn: 对以色列或真主党没有约束力

vocabulary.example_cn: 华盛顿新粉刷的倒影池

sentences.en: The peace talks planned today between the U.S. and Iran now called off, at least according to the Swiss foreign minister.
sentences.cn: 原定于今天举行的美伊和平谈判现已取消，至少瑞士外长是这么说的。
sentences.structure: 主句为'The peace talks...now called off'，过去分词'planned'作后置定语修饰'talks'，'at least according to...'为插入语，补充信息来源。
sentences.analysis: 新闻常用省略be动词的被动结构（called off），'planned today'为压缩定语从句（which were planned today）。

sentences.en: Vice President J.D. Vance was set to travel to Switzerland last night, but the White House said his plans changed last minute, due to unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations.
sentences.cn: 副总统J.D.万斯原定昨晚前往瑞士，但白宫表示，由于下一阶段谈判的后勤问题尚未解决，他的计划在最后一刻发生了变化。
sentences.structure: 并列句由'but'连接；'due to...'为原因状语，'surrounding...'为现在分词短语作定语修饰'logistics'。
sentences.analysis: 'was set to'表示原计划；'last minute'作状语；'unresolved logistics'为新闻压缩表达，意为'未解决的后勤问题'。

sentences.en: And secondly, it did not bind Israel to anything, it did not bind Hezbollah to anything.
sentences.cn: 其次，它没有对以色列施加任何约束，也没有对真主党施加任何约束。
sentences.structure: 两个并列分句，由逗号连接，重复结构强调'bind...to anything'。
sentences.analysis: 'bind...to...'意为'使...受...约束'；重复结构用于强调协议缺乏约束力。

sentences.en: And if Hezbollah attacks Israel, which apparently happened, then Israel responds, they can claim, OK, there's no peace.
sentences.cn: 如果真主党袭击以色列（显然已经发生了），那么以色列就会回应，他们就可以声称：好了，没有和平了。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句'if...'，其中包含非限制性定语从句'which apparently happened'修饰整个从句；主句为'then Israel responds, they can claim...'。
sentences.analysis: 'which apparently happened'为新闻常用插入式定语从句，提供背景信息；'claim'后直接引语。

sentences.en: No. 1 is that it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel.
sentences.cn: 第一，伊朗的目标是在美国和以色列之间制造分裂。
sentences.structure: 主系表结构，表语为that引导的从句；不定式'to sow...'作后置定语修饰'objective'。
sentences.analysis: 'sow divisions'为比喻用法，意为'播下分裂的种子'；'No. 1'为口语化列举。

sentences.en: And hearing that kind of comment from the vice president about Israel is fulfilling that Iranian objective of sowing those kinds of divisions.
sentences.cn: 听到副总统对以色列发表这种言论，恰恰实现了伊朗制造这种分裂的目标。
sentences.structure: 动名词短语'hearing...'作主语，系动词'is'，表语为'fulfilling...'（现在分词作表语）。
sentences.analysis: 'fulfilling that Iranian objective'为现在分词短语作表语，表示'正在实现'；'sowing those kinds of divisions'为动名词短语作介词宾语。

sentences.en: And that rests on deterring what are very real threats to your people.
sentences.cn: 而这有赖于威慑那些对你们人民构成非常真实威胁的因素。
sentences.structure: 主句'that rests on...'，介词'on'后接动名词'deterring'，其宾语为'what'引导的名词性从句。
sentences.analysis: 'rests on'意为'依赖于'；'deterring'后接宾语从句，'what'在从句中作主语。

sentences.en: We may not agree with them as to how they go about that.
sentences.cn: 我们可能不同意他们处理此事的方式。
sentences.structure: 主句'We may not agree with them'，后接'as to'引导的介词短语，'how'引导宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 'as to'意为'关于'；'go about'意为'着手处理'。

sentences.en: And then thirdly, we shouldn't make an assumption that, because there is now this memorandum of understanding with Iran, that everything that is not in the memorandum of understanding is somehow better than it was before.
sentences.cn: 第三，我们不应假设，因为现在有了这份与伊朗的谅解备忘录，所以备忘录中没有涉及的一切都比以前更好。
sentences.structure: 主句'we shouldn't make an assumption'，后接同位语从句'that...'；该从句中包含原因状语从句'because...'，以及主句'that everything...is somehow better...'，其中'everything'后接定语从句'that is not...'。
sentences.analysis: 多重嵌套从句：同位语从句内含原因状语从句和定语从句；'make an assumption that'为常见搭配；'somehow'表示'不知何故'。

sentences.en: Iran funds, supplies, trains, supports terrorist networks all over the world, especially those that are targeting Israel.
sentences.cn: 伊朗资助、供应、训练和支持世界各地的恐怖网络，尤其是那些针对以色列的网络。
sentences.structure: 主句主语'Iran'，后接四个并列谓语动词'funds, supplies, trains, supports'，宾语'terrorist networks'，后接'especially those'作同位语，'that'引导定语从句修饰'those'。
sentences.analysis: 并列动词强调伊朗的多重支持行为；'targeting'为现在分词作定语。

sentences.en: And Sen. Roger Wicker of Mississippi is the top Republican, he was saying, Look, I get that it's not funded by taxpayer dollars, but it's still a payoff.
sentences.cn: 密西西比州的参议员罗杰·威克是共和党高层，他说：听着，我明白这不是用纳税人的钱资助的，但这仍然是一种回报。
sentences.structure: 并列句，第一分句为主系表，第二分句为'he was saying'后接直接引语；直接引语中'Look'为感叹词，'I get that...'为主谓宾从句，'but'连接转折。
sentences.analysis: 'get'意为'理解'；'payoff'意为'回报、贿赂'，此处为贬义。

sentences.en: And he's saying it's a payoff that would make Obama's 2015 deal look like a pittance by comparison.
sentences.cn: 他说，这种回报会让奥巴马2015年的协议相比之下显得微不足道。
sentences.structure: 主句'he's saying'后接宾语从句；从句中'it's a payoff'为主系表，后接定语从句'that would make...'，其中包含'look like a pittance'作宾语补足语。
sentences.analysis: 'make...look like...'为'使...看起来像...'；'pittance'意为'微薄的钱'；'by comparison'为固定短语。

sentences.en: That's the one part of this thing that is the most mystifying at all: why there's any money changing hands here, whether it's taxpayers or not.
sentences.cn: 这是整件事中最令人费解的一点：为什么这里会有任何资金易手，无论是不是纳税人的钱。
sentences.structure: 主句'That's the one part...'，后接定语从句'that is...'；冒号后为同位语从句'why...'，其中包含'whether...or not'让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 'mystifying'意为'令人困惑的'；'changing hands'为固定短语，意为'易手、转手'。

sentences.en: And what you have to look at here is where we were in January, was Iran's nuclear program had been bombed and substantially destroyed.
sentences.cn: 这里你必须看到的是，一月份的情况是伊朗的核计划已被轰炸并基本摧毁。
sentences.structure: 主句主语为'what'引导的名词性从句，系动词'is'，表语为'where'引导的名词性从句；'where'从句中又包含'was Iran's nuclear program had been bombed...'，其中'was'后为省略that的表语从句。
sentences.analysis: 多重名词性从句嵌套；'substantially'意为'基本上'。

sentences.en: And there is some Iranian insistence that they have some control over the state of -- the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 伊朗坚持认为他们对霍尔木兹海峡的状况有一定的控制权。
sentences.structure: 主句'There is some Iranian insistence'，后接同位语从句'that they have...'。
sentences.analysis: 'insistence'后接同位语从句说明具体内容；'have control over'意为'对...有控制权'。

sentences.en: But maybe it was worth it to get out of the military conflict, to get our forces home, to bring down oil prices, to restore some stability in the global economy.
sentences.cn: 但也许这是值得的，为了摆脱军事冲突，让我们的军队回家，降低油价，恢复全球经济的些许稳定。
sentences.structure: 主句'it was worth it'，后接四个并列的不定式短语作目的状语。
sentences.analysis: 'worth it'为固定搭配；并列不定式列举多个目的，增强说服力。

sentences.en: They are setting terms over the Strait of Hormuz, they are setting terms for future negotiations about nuclear weapons.
sentences.cn: 他们在霍尔木兹海峡问题上设定条件，他们为未来关于核武器的谈判设定条件。
sentences.structure: 两个并列分句，结构对称，重复'setting terms'。
sentences.analysis: 'set terms'意为'设定条件'；'over'和'for'分别表示'关于'和'为'。

sentences.en: And as we saw today, they're also insisting on demands about Lebanon that is mentioned in the agreement but has no binding effect on Israel or Hezbollah.
sentences.cn: 正如我们今天所见，他们还坚持关于黎巴嫩的要求，这些要求在协议中有所提及，但对以色列或真主党没有约束力。
sentences.structure: 主句'they're also insisting on demands'，后接定语从句'that is mentioned...but has...'修饰'demands'；句首'as we saw today'为方式状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 'insist on'意为'坚持'；'binding effect'意为'约束力'；定语从句中'that'指代'demands'，但谓语用单数'is'，可能为口语化表达或笔误。

topics.title: 美伊谅解备忘录与'无条件投降'争议
topics.content: 2026年6月，美国与伊朗达成一份谅解备忘录（Memorandum of Understanding, MOU），旨在开启后续谈判。特朗普总统称其为'无条件投降'（unconditional surrender），但协议内容模糊且不完整，未约束以色列或真主党（Hezbollah）。伊朗仍控制霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）并设定谈判条件，引发外界质疑。
topics.keywords: MOU · unconditional surrender · Strait of Hormuz · Hezbollah

topics.title: 美以关系紧张与伊朗分裂策略
topics.content: 副总统万斯（J.D. Vance）批评以色列极右翼官员本-格维尔（Ben-Gvir）和斯莫特里赫（Smotrich）反对美伊协议，称以色列不能'靠杀戮解决所有安全问题'。前大使沃尔克指出，伊朗的长期目标是在美以之间制造分裂（sow divisions），万斯的言论无意中助长了这一策略。

topics.title: 共和党对伊朗重建基金的担忧
topics.content: 美伊协议中包含一个3000亿美元的潜在重建与发展基金（reconstruction fund），由海湾国家组织。共和党参议员如科宁、科顿、恩斯特和克鲁兹普遍担忧，认为这实质上是变相赎金，甚至比奥巴马2015年协议更糟。前大使沃尔克称资金转移'令人费解'（mystifying）。
topics.keywords: reconstruction fund · sanctions · pittance · theocratic lunatics

topics.title: 华盛顿倒影池漆面剥落事件
topics.content: 华盛顿国家广场的林肯纪念堂倒影池（Reflecting Pool）新粉刷后出现蓝色材料剥落（peeling）现象。这一环境问题引发公众关注，可能暗示浅水区存在深层问题，如水质或涂料质量。

quiz.question: 原文中 'the MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender' 中的 'MOU' 指什么？
quiz.explanation: MOU 是 Memorandum of Understanding 的缩写，意为'谅解备忘录'，指初步的非约束性协议。原文中记者质疑该备忘录并非无条件投降，特朗普则坚持称其'实际上可能是'。

quiz.question: 原文 'it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel' 中 'sow divisions' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 'sow divisions' 是固定搭配，意为'制造分裂'，常用于描述挑拨离间的行为。此处指伊朗试图破坏美以关系。

quiz.question: 原文 'would make Obama's 2015 deal look like a pittance by comparison' 中 'pittance' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'pittance' 指'少量、微薄的数额'，常形容钱少得可怜。此处参议员维克尔用其对比，暗示新协议金额巨大，使旧协议相形见绌。

quiz.question: 根据对话，为什么原定于2026年6月19日的美伊和平谈判被取消？
options: B. 以色列空袭导致局势紧张
C. 副总统万斯行程因后勤问题改变
quiz.explanation: 原文明确提到：'Vice President J.D. Vance was set to travel to Switzerland last night, but the White House said his plans changed last minute, due to unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations.' 因此取消原因是万斯行程的后勤问题。

quiz.question: 前大使沃尔克认为美伊协议中哪一点最令人费解？
options: B. 协议未约束以色列
C. 伊朗获得数百亿美元资金
D. 霍尔木兹海峡仍被封锁
quiz.explanation: 沃尔克说：'That's the one part of this thing that is the most mystifying at all: why there's any money changing hands here'，并指出伊朗通过解除制裁和重建基金获得数百亿美元，这令人费解。

quiz.question: 副总统万斯对以色列极右翼官员的回应，被前大使沃尔克如何评价？
options: B. 无意中帮助了伊朗
quiz.explanation: 沃尔克指出：'it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel. And hearing that kind of comment from the vice president about Israel is fulfilling that Iranian objective.' 即万斯的言论无意中帮助伊朗实现了分裂美以的目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Danny Freeman
paragraphs.text: You know, we'll end the morning on a good note. I love it. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Danny Freeman. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 我们会以愉快的方式结束今早的节目。我喜欢这样。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是丹尼·弗里曼。CNN《今晨》节目，由奥蒂·科尼什主持，现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Did Iran surrender to the U.S.? Well, President Trump thinks so.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗向美国投降了吗？嗯，特朗普总统是这么认为的。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: The MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: 这份谅解备忘录看起来不像是无条件投降。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: Well, it really probably is unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，实际上很可能就是无条件投降。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's something blue peeling from the bottom of D.C.'s newly painted Reflecting Pool. So, are these new signs of deep trouble in the shallow green water?
paragraphs.cn: 华盛顿特区新粉刷的倒影池底部有蓝色剥落。那么，这些是浅绿色水域深处麻烦的新迹象吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Let's -- let's be honest. The tone of the administration is not consistently a Christian tone.
paragraphs.cn: 老实说，这届政府的基调并非始终是基督教的基调。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: J.D. Vance confronted by a conservative Catholic podcaster, the vice president defending the administration's values. And is Joe Rogan the new Johnny Carson? What America's most popular podcaster has that the King of Late Night never did.
paragraphs.cn: J.D.万斯遭到一位保守派天主教播客的质问，副总统为政府的价值观辩护。乔·罗根是新的约翰尼·卡森吗？美国最受欢迎的播客有什么是深夜秀之王从未拥有的。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.text: You know, I've seen skeptics of the deal. People say the Iranians will never change their behavior. Well, maybe that's true. And if so, they don't get any of the benefits of the bargain. But isn't it worth trying?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我见过对该协议的怀疑者。人们说伊朗人永远不会改变他们的行为。嗯，也许这是真的。如果是这样，他们就不会得到协议的任何好处。但难道不值得一试吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The U.S. and Iran giving peace a chance. And yet, just one day into it, could it already be on shaky ground? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and here's where we begin: this breaking news. The peace talks planned today between the U.S. and Iran now called off, at least according to the Swiss foreign minister. Vice President J.D.
paragraphs.cn: 美国和伊朗给和平一个机会。然而，仅仅过了一天，它是否已经岌岌可危？大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什，我们从这里开始：这条突发新闻。原定今天举行的美伊和平谈判现已取消，至少据瑞士外长所说。副总统J.D.万斯原定昨晚前往瑞士，但白宫表示，他的计划在最后一刻改变，原因是下一阶段谈判的后勤问题尚未解决。今早，海上封锁已解除。霍尔木兹海峡在技术上已开放。时间紧迫。还有60天达成最终协议。然而，特朗普总统的这番话引起了一些人的惊讶。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Vance was set to travel to Switzerland last night, but the White House said his plans changed last minute, due to unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations. This morning, the naval blockade has been lifted. The Strait of Hormuz is technically open. The clock is ticking. Sixty days to get a final deal. President Trump is raising some eyebrows, however, with this.
paragraphs.cn: 万斯原定昨晚前往瑞士，但白宫表示，他的计划在最后一刻改变，原因是下一阶段谈判的后勤问题尚未解决。今早，海上封锁已解除。霍尔木兹海峡在技术上已开放。时间紧迫。还有60天达成最终协议。然而，特朗普总统的这番话引起了一些人的惊讶。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: You had talked about you only wanted unconditional surrender and --
paragraphs.cn: 你曾说过你只想要无条件投降 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: -- the MOU doesn't look like unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 但这份谅解备忘录看起来不像无条件投降。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: Well, it really probably is unconditional surrender.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，它很可能实际上就是无条件投降。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining me now, Kurt Volker, former U.S. ambassador to NATO. Thank you for being here this morning. First, I want to just start with the talks being called off today. Is that bad news? How should we read that?
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我的是前美国驻北约大使库尔特·沃尔克。感谢您今早来到这里。首先，我想从今天谈判被取消开始。这是坏消息吗？我们应该如何解读？

paragraphs.speaker: Kurt Volker
paragraphs.text: Well, good morning, Audie. And look, I think we had to expect a lot of bumps in the road, because this is a very vague and very partial agreement. It is meant to be the start of negotiations, rather than the end of negotiations. And secondly, it did not bind Israel to anything. It did not bind Hezbollah to anything. So, Iran now has a card to play, which is peace in Lebanon. And if Hezbollah attacks Israel, which apparently happened, then Israel responds, they can claim, OK, there's no peace.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，早上好，奥迪。听着，我认为我们必须预料到会有很多波折，因为这是一个非常模糊且非常片面的协议。它旨在成为谈判的开始，而不是结束。其次，它没有约束以色列任何东西，也没有约束真主党任何东西。所以，伊朗现在有一张牌可打，那就是黎巴嫩的和平。如果真主党攻击以色列 --  -- 显然发生了 --  -- 那么以色列回应，他们就可以声称，好吧，没有和平。

paragraphs.speaker: Kurt Volker
paragraphs.text: So, we don't have to do anything. So, there's a lot to play out here.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们不必做任何事。因此，这里有很多变数。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. You just mentioned that. Last night, at least 16 people, actually, were killed after Israeli airstrikes. Vice President Vance had pretty strong words for members of the Israeli government, who have criticized the agreement with Iran and him. Here's what he had to say.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。你刚才提到了。昨晚，至少16人在以色列空袭后丧生。副总统万斯对批评与伊朗协议及其本人的以色列政府成员发表了相当强硬的言论。以下是他所说的话。

paragraphs.speaker: Vance
paragraphs.text: I don't think Bibi himself has actually criticized the deal, because I think he's maybe a little bit more familiar with the details of what's in it. But, yes, you've seen people in their system -- Ben-Gvir and Smotrich -- who've attacked the deal. And I guess my response to them would be, what is your exact proposal? And, you know, you're a country of -- of 9 million people. You can't just kill your way out of solving every single national security problem that you have.
paragraphs.cn: 我不认为比比本人实际上批评了该协议，因为我认为他可能对协议中的细节更熟悉一些。但是，是的，你看到他们体系内的人 --  -- 本-格维尔和斯莫特里奇 --  -- 攻击了该协议。我想我对他们的回应是，你们的具体提议是什么？而且，你知道，你们是一个拥有900万人口的国家。你不能仅仅通过杀戮来解决每一个国家安全问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: As a diplomat, how do you hear how he's defending this?
paragraphs.cn: 作为一名外交官，你如何理解他这样辩护？

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: Well, there are several things that come to mind. No. 1 is that it is an Iranian objective to sow divisions between the United States and Israel. And hearing that kind of comment from the vice president about Israel is fulfilling that Iranian objective of sowing those kinds of divisions. Secondly, if you're Israel, you're living in a very dangerous neighborhood, and what you need to do is provide security for your people. And that rests on deterring what are very real threats to your people.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，有几件事浮现在脑海中。第一，伊朗的目标是在美国和以色列之间制造分裂。听到副总统对以色列发表这样的评论，正好实现了伊朗制造这种分裂的目标。第二，如果你是以色列，你生活在一个非常危险的地区，你需要做的是为你的人民提供安全。而这取决于威慑对你的人民构成的非常真实的威胁。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: So, Israel is focused on that. We may not agree with them as to how they go about that. We may think sometimes they are too aggressive in pursuing that. But that is what the Israeli prime minister's job is, and he's not going to be deterred from that. We have to look at that reality. And then thirdly, we shouldn't make an assumption that, because there is now this memorandum of understanding with Iran, that everything that is not in the memorandum of understanding is somehow better than it was before.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，以色列专注于这一点。我们可能不同意他们处理此事的方式。我们有时可能认为他们在追求这一目标时过于激进。但这就是以色列总理的职责所在，他不会因此被吓倒。我们必须正视这一现实。第三，我们不应因为现在与伊朗达成了谅解备忘录，就认为备忘录之外的一切都比以前更好。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: Iran funds, supplies, trains, supports terrorist networks all over the world, especially those that are targeting Israel. And we have to recognize that that is real.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗资助、供应、训练和支持世界各地的恐怖网络，尤其是那些针对以色列的。我们必须认识到这是事实。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And I think a lot of people do, frankly. The people who are loudest in their complaints about this deal are Republicans. And we're just hearing so many senators saying similar things, even, to what you might hear from those loud voices in Israel. I want to play for you a series of voices. SEN. JOHN CORNYN (R-TX): Everything I've heard about it causes me concern. SEN. TOM COTTON (R-AR): I do have concerns that certain aspects of this deal might be a step in the wrong direction. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且我认为很多人确实如此。对这项协议抱怨最响的是共和党人。我们听到很多参议员在说类似的话，甚至与你在以色列听到的那些响亮声音相似。我想播放一系列声音给你听。参议员约翰·科宁（德克萨斯州共和党人）：我所听到的一切都让我担忧。参议员汤姆·科顿（阿肯色州共和党人）：我确实担心该协议的某些方面可能是朝着错误方向迈出的一步。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: JONI ERNST (R-IA): I have to know where that money is coming from, because I don't think my constituents are going to be really happy about it, if that's all U.S. taxpayer dollars. SEN. TED CRUZ (R-TX): History demonstrates that giving billions of dollars to theocratic lunatics who want to murder us is an exceptionally bad idea.
paragraphs.cn: 参议员乔尼·恩斯特（爱荷华州共和党人）：我必须知道这笔钱从哪里来，因为如果全是美国纳税人的钱，我不认为我的选民会对此感到高兴。参议员特德·克鲁兹（德克萨斯州共和党人）：历史表明，给想要谋杀我们的神权疯子数十亿美元是一个非常糟糕的主意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, people are kind of up in arms about many things, but specifically, the $300 billion potential reconstruction and development fund, which is supposed to be an investment fund. And Sen. Roger Wicker of Mississippi is the top Republican. He was saying, Look, I get that it's not funded by taxpayer dollars, but it's still a payoff. And he's saying it's a payoff that would make Obama's 2015 deal look like a pittance by comparison.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，人们对很多事情都感到愤怒，但具体来说，是3000亿美元的潜在重建和发展基金，这应该是一个投资基金。密西西比州的参议员罗杰·威克是共和党高层。他说，我明白这不是由纳税人的钱资助的，但这仍然是一种贿赂。他还说，与这笔贿赂相比，奥巴马2015年的协议简直是小巫见大巫。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: Yes, that's exactly right. That's the one part of this thing that is the most mystifying at all: why there's any money changing hands here, whether it's taxpayers or not. It doesn't matter.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，完全正确。这是整件事中最令人费解的部分：为什么这里会有任何资金易手，无论是否来自纳税人。这并不重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But they're saying there isn't, right? They're arguing that money isn't changing hands.
paragraphs.cn: 但他们说没有，对吧？他们辩称没有资金易手。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: Well, they say that, but then you look, and you say sanctions are being lifted. Iran is getting oil sales. There will be a reconstruction fund that the Gulf states will organize. That's money. And what you have to look at here is where we were in January, was Iran's nuclear program had been bombed and substantially destroyed. And the Gulf [SIC] of Hormuz was open. Now, we had four months of war that we paid for, and the nuclear program remains destroyed.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他们这么说，但你看，制裁正在解除。伊朗正在获得石油销售。海湾国家将组织一个重建基金。这就是钱。你必须看到的是，今年一月，伊朗的核计划已被轰炸并基本摧毁。霍尔木兹海峡是开放的。现在，我们经历了四个月的战争，我们为此付出了代价，核计划仍然被摧毁。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: And we are still in an open-ended negotiation about where that goes. And there is some Iranian insistence that they have some control over the state of -- the Strait of Hormuz. OK. But maybe it was worth it to get out of the military conflict, to get our forces home, to bring down oil prices, to restore some stability in the global economy. You can argue, OK, we can go back and reset. But then you add on top of that, and Iran gets access to hundreds of billions of dollars. That's kind of inexplicable.
paragraphs.cn: 我们仍在就事态走向进行无休止的谈判。伊朗坚持要求对霍尔木兹海峡的状况拥有一定控制权。好吧。但也许摆脱军事冲突、让我们的部队回家、降低油价、恢复全球经济的稳定是值得的。你可以说，好吧，我们可以回去重新开始。但在此基础上，伊朗还能获得数千亿美元。这有点难以解释。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, none of this sounds like what the president said: unconditional surrender by Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这些听起来都不像总统所说的：伊朗无条件投降。

paragraphs.speaker: Volker
paragraphs.text: Well, I know that's what he wants to say, but the Iranian regime is still in place. They are setting terms over the Strait of Hormuz. They are setting terms for future negotiations about nuclear weapons. And as we saw today, they're also insisting on demands about Lebanon that is mentioned in the agreement but has no binding effect on Israel or Hezbollah.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我知道他想说什么，但伊朗政权仍然存在。他们在霍尔木兹海峡设定条件。他们为未来关于核武器的谈判设定条件。正如我们今天所见，他们还坚持对黎巴嫩的要求，这些要求在协议中有所提及，但对以色列或真主党没有约束力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's Ambassador Kurt Volker. Thank you so much for your time.
paragraphs.cn: 这位是库尔特·沃尔克大使。非常感谢您抽出时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, accused killer Luigi Mangione changes his mind. His lawyers announcing another new defense strategy this week. And the Reflecting Pool here on the National Mall got a multimillion- dollar makeover. But does President Trump's fix now need some fixing? Plus, today is Juneteenth, and the Juneteenth flag is now flying over Wisconsin's state capitol for the seventh year in a row. We'll be right back.
paragraphs.cn: 在CNN《今晨》节目中，被指控的凶手路易吉·曼吉奥内改变了主意。他的律师本周宣布了另一项新的辩护策略。国家广场上的倒影池进行了耗资数百万美元的改造。但特朗普总统的修复现在是否需要一些修补？另外，今天是六月节，六月节旗帜连续第七年在威斯康星州议会大厦上空飘扬。我们马上回来。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: It looks bad. I just see green. Green slime.
paragraphs.cn: 看起来糟透了。我只看到绿色。绿色黏液。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Pouring all that peroxide into it clearly didn't help. I feel for the ducks.
paragraphs.cn: 把那么多过氧化氢倒进去显然没有帮助。我同情那些鸭子。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. We could call it "Watergate." A new issue after the multi-million-dollar renovation of the Reflecting Pool. Now we're going to show you this: blue material peeling off the bottom. It's not clear yet if that's paint or sealant. And earlier this week, crews were working on the algae turning the pool green. Despite these images, the U.S. Department of the Interior insists that the water is crystal-clear and that crews are just vacuuming up dead algae.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。我们可以称之为"水门事件"。倒影池耗资数百万美元翻新后出现的新问题。现在我们要给你看这个：蓝色材料从底部剥落。目前还不清楚那是油漆还是密封剂。本周早些时候，工作人员正在处理使水池变绿的藻类。尽管有这些图片，美国内政部坚称水质清澈透明，工作人员只是在清理死藻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Quote, "just like the destroyed Iranian navy resting on the bottom of the Persian Gulf." Again, that's the word from the Department of the Interior in charge of the Reflecting Pool. Joining me now in the group chat, Elliot Williams, CNN legal analyst and former federal prosecutor; Michael Warren, politics editor for "The Dispatch"; and Lulu Garcia-Navarro, CNN contributor and host of "The New York Times" podcast, "The Interview." All right.
paragraphs.cn: 引用，"就像被摧毁的伊朗海军躺在波斯湾底部一样。"再次强调，这是负责倒影池的内政部的话。现在加入我们群聊的有：CNN法律分析师、前联邦检察官埃利奥特·威廉姆斯；《电讯报》政治编辑迈克尔·沃伦；以及CNN撰稿人、《纽约时报》播客《访谈》主持人露露·加西亚-纳瓦罗。好了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, both the Reflecting Pool and the Iran war are a win, which if you were here for my interview with Ambassador Volker just now, I'm not sure that's a great analogy. But I want to point out: at least two companies worked on this project. And public records show that an Ohio company, Greenwater Services, was hired to install the filtration system, and a Virginia- based Atlantic Industrial Coatings to resurface it.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，倒影池和伊朗战争都是胜利，如果你刚才听了我和沃尔克大使的采访，我不确定这是个很好的类比。但我想指出：至少有两家公司参与了该项目。公共记录显示，俄亥俄州的Greenwater Services公司被雇来安装过滤系统，而弗吉尼亚州的Atlantic Industrial Coatings公司负责重新铺设表面。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And even though Greenwater Services didn't respond to our request for comment, the owner of Atlantic Industrial Coatings did. They said -- Eddie Wood, he's -- who owns Atlantic Industrial Coatings, said, "The images do not provide enough information to tell exactly what this is. There are several things that we've got to address when we come back for maintenance, and anything like that will be addressed if it's a problem."
paragraphs.cn: 尽管Greenwater Services没有回应我们的评论请求，但Atlantic Industrial Coatings的老板回应了。他们说 --  -- 埃迪·伍德，他是Atlantic Industrial Coatings的老板，说："这些图片没有提供足够的信息来确切判断这是什么。我们回来进行维护时需要处理几件事，如果有问题，任何类似的事情都会得到处理。"

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: So first of all, the fact that it was called "Greenwater" should have been a giveaway. I'm just saying you can't make that up. Sorry. Go on. MICHAEL WARREN, POLITICS EDITOR, "THE DISPATCH": No. No,
paragraphs.cn: 所以首先，它被称为"Greenwater"应该是个线索。我只是说这可不是编的。抱歉。请继续。迈克尔·沃伦，《电讯报》政治编辑：不。不，

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: I had to make the joke.
paragraphs.cn: 我不得不开这个玩笑。

paragraphs.speaker: Elliot Williams
paragraphs.text: Delivered well. It was good.
paragraphs.cn: 讲得不错。挺好的。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: No, look, I -- I think this is one of these things that was thoroughly predictable, if you read anything, which you know, I know nothing about water filtration. But I was reading about this as the administration was pushing this narrative that they were finally cleaning it up. Is that this is a long-standing problem in the Reflecting Pool, that these algae blooms happen, because there just isn't enough, you know, water flow out of -- out of it.
paragraphs.cn: 不，你看，我认为这件事完全可以预见，如果你读过任何相关报道的话 --  -- 你知道，我对水过滤一无所知。但我在政府大力宣传他们终于要清理水池的时候读到了这些。这是倒影池长期存在的问题，这些藻类大量繁殖，因为水流不够充分。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: And I do think that, you know, two weeks ago, a week ago on social media, you had all these kind of supporters of the president, Republicans saying, finally, the president is cleaning this up. And it was just -- it was obvious that this was going --
paragraphs.cn: 而且我确实认为，两周前、一周前在社交媒体上，总统的支持者们、共和党人都在说，总统终于要清理这个水池了。很明显，这将会 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: This was very likely to happen. Now, maybe they're just cleaning up dead algae, maybe just the seal is coming off. That doesn't --
paragraphs.cn: 这很可能会发生。现在，也许他们只是在清理死藻，也许只是密封层脱落了。这并不 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, just because I went down the rabbit hole on this. Once you start, you know, hand pouring jugs --
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，只是因为我深入研究了这个问题。一旦你开始，你知道，用手倒几加仑 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- of peroxide in there, and it kills the algae bloom, it floats to the bottom. And now you've got to scrape it up.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 过氧化氢进去，它杀死了藻华，藻类沉到池底。现在你得把它刮起来。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: I feel the ducks, too.
paragraphs.cn: 我也为鸭子感到难过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the ducks. which now --
paragraphs.cn: 还有鸭子，它们现在 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Just the ducks, not the ducts.
paragraphs.cn: 只是鸭子，不是管道。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, what do you think of this?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，你怎么看这件事？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: You know, it's -- one, it's a question of did this need to happen in the way it did? And perhaps -- right? -- perhaps there -- we all live in Washington, D.C., or around the area. You see the algae there. There could have been a benefit to it. Once again, it is the Trump -- the Trump and the folks around him making the case aggressively before it, almost as a moral matter. And that when it goes badly now, sort of it's adding to the face (ph), right?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，这 --  -- 首先，问题是这件事有必要以这种方式发生吗？也许 --  -- 对吧？ --  -- 也许 --  -- 我们都住在华盛顿特区或周边地区。你看到那里的藻类了。它本来可能是有益的。再说一次，是特朗普 --  -- 特朗普和他身边的人在之前就极力主张这件事，几乎把它当作一个道德问题。而现在事情变糟了，这有点让局面更难看，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, it was like a metaphor for the cleanup of Washington.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish

paragraphs.cn: 嗯，现在它花了很多钱。基本上，1400万美元的价格标签并不是我们最初预想的。我想给你播放特朗普是如何向公众推销这件事的。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: One point nine million. One week. We had estimates to fix it of about $355 million. And it was going to take three and a half years. You can see by the size of it. And so, we're going to be able to do it for about a million eight -- 1.8 million -- and it's going to take one week. And we'll be finished, I guess.
paragraphs.cn: 190万。一周。我们之前估计修复费用大约3.55亿美元，需要三年半时间。你可以从它的规模看出来。所以，我们能够以大约180万 --  -- 180万美元 --  -- 完成，只需要一周时间。我想我们就能完工了。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: You see, that's the point I'm making. He went so far out there making the case as to how quickly it was going to happen, how cheaply it would be done, and how perfect it would be that what could have been an honest problem of, you know, maybe a piece chipped off. People who have been in a swimming pool know that that happens from time to time. What could have been a simple problem now becomes an embarrassment. And it's worse.
paragraphs.cn: 你看，这就是我要说的。他走得太远了，极力宣称这件事会多快完成、多便宜、多完美，而本来这可能只是一个诚实的问题，比如，可能有一块碎片脱落。去过游泳池的人都知道这种事时有发生。本来可能是一个简单的问题，现在却成了尴尬。而且更糟了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. It's been interesting seeing progressives sort of have fun with this analogy. Remember, the Interior is the one that brought up the comparison to the Iran War. paragraphs.speaker: Cornish

I come to this not only as somebody who goes to concerts, but also as somebody who sings with a band called Pink Martini. And we say, yes, use your phones, please don't use your flash.

A show likes the "The Summer I Turned Pretty" on Amazon. The book sold 4.5 million copies and on the first seven days of airing, 25 million streams. The money's there. ARI SHAPIRO, CO-HOST, "ENGAGEMENT PARTY"
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。所以不用说，数据很好。看看像《激烈竞争》这样的节目，这本书卖了130万册，每集流媒体播放量1000万。像亚马逊上的《我变美的那个夏天》，这本书卖了450万册，播出头七天流媒体播放量2500万。钱就在那里。阿里·夏皮罗，《订婚派对》联合主持人。

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: The money's there. And what I think is really interesting, which we discussed in the latest episode of Engagement Party, is the way these romance shows depict masculinity and the male heroes. And it is not the kind of tough guy, stuff your feelings down, punch a wall depiction. It's actual communication and consideration of people's feelings. And it doesn't feel dutiful.
paragraphs.cn: 钱就在那里。我认为真正有趣的是，我们在最新一期的《订婚派对》中讨论过，这些爱情剧如何描绘男性气质和男性英雄。这不是那种硬汉、压抑情感、砸墙的描绘。而是真正的沟通和对他人感受的考虑。而且这并不让人觉得是出于义务。

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: It still feels romantic and sexy, and clearly it's working because people are watching these shows, but it's a different kind of depiction of male --
paragraphs.cn: 它仍然让人觉得浪漫和性感，显然这很有效，因为人们在看这些剧，但这是对男性的一种不同描绘 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yeah, and also there's male fans. Like, I remember during the "Heated Rivalry" thing, like, all the hockey guys who got into that show, which was, you know, a vibe.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，而且还有男性粉丝。比如，我记得在《激烈竞争》那件事中，所有那些冰球运动员都迷上了那部剧，你知道，那是一种氛围。

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: It's for everyone. I mean, I think this is a tale as old as time, frankly. I mean, where do you get these stories? And -- and --
paragraphs.cn: 这是给所有人的。我的意思是，坦率地说，我认为这是一个古老的故事。我是说，这些故事从何而来？而且 --  -- 而且 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But romance novels were sort of in the ghetto of genres. Like, people were like, ugh.
paragraphs.cn: 但言情小说曾经是类型中的贫民窟。就像，人们会觉得，呃。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: They were. They were, but I think that that was a misunderstanding. As a longtime romance reader and a longtime reader of romantasy --
paragraphs.cn: 它们曾经是，但我认为那是一种误解。作为一个长期的言情小说读者和长期的浪漫奇幻读者 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are you pounding the table right now? (LAUGHTER)
paragraphs.cn: 你现在是在拍桌子吗？（笑声）

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: I am because I feel very strongly and I'm very invested in this. Female rage is something that doesn't get enough stuff. (CROSSTALK)
paragraphs.cn: 是的，因为我的感受非常强烈，而且我对此投入很深。女性的愤怒没有得到足够的关注。（交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: But you know, I do think that this is a genre that gets pooh-poohed by in elite circles, but has always been the engine of publishing. Women read. Women are the biggest readers, book clubs. And these are the stories that, you know, that we enjoy for all sorts of reasons.
paragraphs.cn: 但你知道，我确实认为这是一个在精英圈子里被嗤之以鼻的类型，但它一直是出版业的引擎。女性阅读。女性是最大的读者群体，还有读书俱乐部。而这些故事，你知道，我们出于各种原因喜欢它们。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.text: But we have seen Hollywood as invested as they are now.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们看到好莱坞像现在这样投入。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: I agree, but I don't think they understood how to make them in the way that you get the nuance in the books, right? In the books, you're getting character development, and then it all would get flattened off into Harlequin romance type stuff. The big subtext here is the social media is everything, right? I don't know if we wouldn't be here at all without these folks on TikTok. But again, you're talking about literally tens of millions of people promoting books and sharing them and so on. There we go.
paragraphs.cn: 我同意，但我认为他们不知道如何以捕捉书中细微差别的方式来制作它们，对吧？在书中，你会看到角色发展，然后这一切都被扁平化成了那种通俗浪漫小说。这里的大背景是社交媒体就是一切，对吧？如果没有TikTok上的这些人，我不知道我们是否还会在这里。但再说一次，你谈论的是数千万人推广书籍、分享书籍等等。就是这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Michael Warren
paragraphs.text: What's old is new again. Sex sells. This is, like, a shocking idea.
paragraphs.cn: 旧瓶装新酒。性吸引人。这真是个惊人的想法。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: It's not just sex. It's the romance.
paragraphs.cn: 不仅仅是性，还有浪漫。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And we end the segment with the men arguing about how much sex is in the romance genre. Ari, thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 我们以男人们争论浪漫题材中有多少性内容来结束这一环节。Ari，非常感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Love it. We've got new episodes of "Engagement Party" every Friday. Check us out. And in the meantime, we're going to lawyer up. Elliot's talking about guns and marijuana, why this latest Supreme Court decision could reach far beyond one man's case, and today's talks with Iran, the formal signing ceremony that's been canceled at the last minute.
paragraphs.cn: 太棒了。我们的《订婚派对》每周五都有新集。请关注我们。与此同时，我们要找律师了。Elliot在谈论枪支和大麻，为什么最高法院的最新裁决可能远远超出一个人的案件，以及今天与伊朗的会谈，正式签字仪式在最后一刻被取消。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. I want to thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is half past the hour. And we're following this breaking news. Overnight, today's planned talks between Iran and the U.S. canceled. Vice President delaying his trip to Switzerland at the last minute because of what the White House is calling unresolved logistics surrounding the next phase of negotiations. And U.S. boat strikes on suspected drug-smuggling boats still happening.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是Audie Cornish。感谢您收看CNN今晨节目。现在是半点。我们正在关注这条突发新闻。昨晚，原定今天举行的伊朗与美国会谈被取消。副总统在最后一刻推迟了前往瑞士的行程，白宫称原因是下一阶段谈判的后勤问题尚未解决。美国对涉嫌贩毒船只的打击仍在继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The latest was yesterday, and three people were killed. At least 211 people have been killed in the Trump administration's war on alleged drug boats. Some lawmakers are calling for an investigation, saying the administration is not offering enough evidence to support its claims. And today, on Juneteenth, the Obama Presidential Center is set to open to the public. Yesterday, the Obamas celebrated the grand opening. Former Presidents Clinton, Bush, and Biden were there to support.
paragraphs.cn: 最新一起发生在昨天，造成三人死亡。在特朗普政府对涉嫌贩毒船只的战争中，至少有211人丧生。一些议员呼吁进行调查，称政府没有提供足够证据支持其说法。今天，在六月节，奥巴马总统中心将向公众开放。昨天，奥巴马夫妇庆祝了盛大开幕。前总统克林顿、布什和拜登到场支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the former first lady kicked off the celebration honoring what she called her husband's dedication to the American people.
paragraphs.cn: 前第一夫人启动了庆祝活动，以纪念她所说的丈夫对美国人民的奉献。

paragraphs.speaker: Michelle Obama
paragraphs.text: Yeah, you were unflappable at every turn, always focused, always calm, always looking at the long view.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，你在每一个转折点都镇定自若，始终专注，始终冷静，总是着眼于长远。

paragraphs.speaker: Barack Obama
paragraphs.text: Democracy can be frustrating. It can be slow. It can be inefficient. And yet, more than anything, I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of just how special, how precious our democracy truly is.
paragraphs.cn: 民主有时令人沮丧。它可能缓慢。它可能效率低下。然而，最重要的是，我希望这个中心能够证明我们的民主是多么特别、多么珍贵。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, general admission tickets for the center are sold out through the end of October. And the Supreme Court expanding gun rights to some who light up. The High Court siding with a Texas man who argued that he shouldn't be criminally charged for owning a gun just because he smokes marijuana. The decision, loosening a federal ban, but the ruling is not so cut and dry, which is why it's time to lawyer up with CNN legal analyst Elliot Williams. Okay, you started out a baby federal prosecutor once upon a time.
paragraphs.cn: 目前，该中心的普通门票已售罄至十月底。最高法院扩大了部分吸毒者的持枪权利。法院支持了一名得克萨斯州男子的观点，他认为自己仅仅因为吸食大麻就不应因拥有枪支而受到刑事指控。这一裁决放宽了联邦禁令，但判决并非一刀切，因此是时候请CNN法律分析师埃利奥特·威廉姆斯来解读了。好吧，你曾经是一名初级联邦检察官。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Baby federal prosecutor once upon a time.
paragraphs.cn: 曾经是一名初级联邦检察官。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I kind of think of prosecutors liking a good drug charge here and there.
paragraphs.cn: 我有点觉得检察官喜欢时不时地处理一些毒品指控。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: And one of the more common things that young prosecutors prosecute is this kind of case, which is a prohibition case where an individual is prohibited from owning a firearm based on the class of society that they belong to. The big ones are felons that's a very common one, but also drug users someone who is a drug user or an addict of any controlled substance can be barred from owning a firearm.
paragraphs.cn: 年轻检察官经常起诉的案件之一就是这类案件，即禁止令案件，其中个人因其所属的社会阶层而被禁止拥有枪支。最常见的是重罪犯，但还有吸毒者 --  -- 任何受控物质的吸毒者或成瘾者都可能被禁止拥有枪支。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Well, this individual who was a casual habitual marijuana user said he used it a few times a week had his firearm taken away and brought a lawsuit saying that -- well, the Second Amendment should not bar someone like me from using a firearm.
paragraphs.cn: 这名男子是一名偶尔习惯性吸食大麻的人，他说自己每周吸几次，他的枪支被没收，于是他提起诉讼，声称第二修正案不应禁止像他这样的人使用枪支。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can you talk about how this is complicated by the moment we're in or recreation racial marijuana is everywhere? I don't even know how you define a casual habitual user.
paragraphs.cn: 你能谈谈这在我们所处的时代或娱乐性大麻普及的情况下是如何变得复杂的吗？我甚至不知道如何定义偶尔习惯性使用者。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Exactly right. And that was exactly -- so it's a unanimous decision, but Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson picked up on that point exactly. She says that the state of our jurisprudence on firearms leaves open these open questions. So how do you define what a casual user of marijuana is? Now, the way they use it is that, number one, it is increasingly in a different class of substance than cocaine, heroin, whatever else, and it just had its classifications level lower. That's what Justice Gorsuch said.
paragraphs.cn: 完全正确。这正是 --  -- 所以这是一致裁决，但凯坦吉·布朗·杰克逊大法官恰恰指出了这一点。她说，我们关于枪支的判例法留下了这些悬而未决的问题。那么，如何定义偶尔吸食大麻的人呢？他们使用的方式是，首先，它越来越属于与可卡因、海洛因等不同的物质类别，并且其分类等级刚刚降低。这是戈萨奇大法官所说的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: But it's a vague standard. And I just think what we're seeing here is the convergence of two big areas in American law, relaxing laws on cannabis, but also relaxing laws on firearms. And the Supreme Court has been heading in that direction in both areas, and they really came to a head.
paragraphs.cn: 但这是一个模糊的标准。我认为我们在这里看到的是美国法律两大领域的交汇：放宽大麻法律，同时放宽枪支法律。最高法院在这两个领域都朝着这个方向发展，现在它们真正碰撞到了一起。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yeah. And so that's the legal part. The political bedfellows of this is also, it's just like really interesting because I think even in Republican circles, there is, let's say, a more relaxed attitude in so many ways towards cannabis for sure, but even the punishment aspect of that.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这就是法律层面。政治上的同床异梦也很有趣，因为我认为即使在共和党内部，对大麻的态度在很多方面都更加宽松，甚至包括惩罚方面。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: And look, I think that if you look at all the different, you know, the opinions and then the concurrences and you had different viewpoints all coming to the same conclusion and I think that is reflected in the fact that it was an 9-0 decision. And it's a reminder as well that a lot of controversial or supposedly controversial issues that there can be and there often is in in the Supreme Court, a lot of nine or eight decisions.
paragraphs.cn: 而且，我认为如果你看看所有不同的意见和同意意见，不同的观点都得出相同的结论，这反映在9-0的裁决中。这也提醒我们，许多有争议或被认为有争议的问题，在最高法院往往会有9-0或8-1的裁决。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The more shocking thing.
paragraphs.cn: 更令人震惊的是。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: No, but I think it's actually it's less shocking than if you follow the court and look at a lot of the reasoning. I mean, even Elena Kagan, a liberal appointed justice, as you know, we're all originalists now, as she said several years ago. I think that there is oftentimes those don't get as much attention. But on an issue like this, I think it does get attention because --
paragraphs.cn: 不，我认为如果你关注法院并审视其推理，这其实并不那么令人震惊。即使是自由派任命的法官埃琳娜·卡根，正如她几年前所说，我们现在都是原旨主义者。我认为这些往往没有得到足够的关注。但在这样的问题上，它确实引起了关注，因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I add this legal footnote?
paragraphs.cn: 我能补充一个法律脚注吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is the same law used against Hunter Biden --
paragraphs.cn: 同样的法律也曾用于亨特·拜登 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- when he was convicted of buying a gun while being a cocaine user in 2018. Now, of course, he was pardoned for all the things.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 他在2018年因作为可卡因使用者购买枪支而被定罪。当然，他后来被赦免了所有罪行。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: There's a few things --
paragraphs.cn: 有几件事 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But it's interesting that this law has been deployed in a very public way.
paragraphs.cn: 但有趣的是，这项法律被以一种非常公开的方式使用。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Yeah, there's a few things going on here. One, to your point, it's the strange bedfellows of pro-gun folks, but also people with different types of drug convictions. Now, they're very careful to say this is just for casual users of marijuana, other people with different sort of substances will still have the prohibition apply to them. I think the Supreme Court was trying to find a way to carve marijuana out of the law. Like the other prohibitions, like being a felon, either you are or you aren't.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这里有几件事。首先，正如你所说，这是支持枪支的人与不同类型毒品定罪者之间的奇怪联盟。他们非常谨慎地表示，这仅适用于大麻的偶尔使用者，其他不同物质的使用者仍将受到禁令约束。我认为最高法院试图找到一种方法将大麻从法律中剔除。而其他禁令，比如重罪犯，要么是，要么不是。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Either you have a conviction or you do not. Here, it's a vague standard of how do you ultimately define when someone is sufficiently dangerous based on the type of substance they're using. And it is a pretty vague standard that they follow.
paragraphs.cn: 要么你有定罪，要么没有。这里，他们依据一种模糊的标准，即如何根据某人使用的物质类型最终定义其是否足够危险。他们遵循的标准相当模糊。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, you guys stay here. Next, we're going to talk about something else. There's a former late-night host with a hot take. We'll talk about why Jay Leno is saying that podcasters are the new king of talk. Plus, there's this.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，你们待在这儿。接下来，我们要谈点别的。一位前深夜秀主持人发表了尖锐观点。我们来聊聊为什么杰·雷诺说播客主是新的脱口秀之王。另外，还有这个。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.text: I'm not saying we're perfect because we're not.
paragraphs.cn: 我并不是说我们完美，因为我们并不完美。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: An interview, the fellow Catholic takes a turn. Vice President Vance pressed on whether the administration practices what it preaches.
paragraphs.cn: 一次采访中，这位天主教徒的言论出现了转折。副总统万斯被追问政府是否言行一致。

paragraphs.speaker: Ross Douthat
paragraphs.text: Let's be honest, the tone of the administration is not consistently a Christian tone. There is a tone of aggressive uncharity to people who aren't on board with the administration's policies.
paragraphs.cn: 老实说，这届政府的基调并非始终是基督教的基调。对于那些不认同政府政策的人，存在一种咄咄逼人的不仁慈态度。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So Vice President Vance sat down for what he probably thought might be a friendly interview with Catholic conservative writer Ross Douthat, but the administration's Christian values were quickly called into question. And here's how Vance pushed back.
paragraphs.cn: 于是副总统万斯坐下来，接受了他可能以为会是友好的采访，对象是天主教保守派作家罗斯·多塞特，但政府的基督教价值观很快受到质疑。以下是万斯的反驳。

paragraphs.speaker: Vance
paragraphs.text: For every clip that you could show me of me or the president or some cabinet secretary saying something that, in your view, is unchristian, I could show you another few clips of us doing something or saying something that is like very Christian. I'm not saying we're perfect because we're not.
paragraphs.cn: 对于你能给我看的任何一段视频，显示我、总统或某位内阁部长说了你认为不基督教的话，我都能给你看另外几段视频，显示我们做了或说了非常基督教的事情。我并不是说我们完美，因为我们并不完美。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this. Michael, you had like a pretty interesting take where you found his answers evasive, which is interesting, but also a poor Christian witness.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。我想谈谈这个。迈克尔，你有一个相当有趣的观点，认为他的回答回避问题，这很有意思，但也是一种糟糕的基督徒见证。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: Look, if I were to walk into my confessional and tell the priest, oh, I've committed these sins, but you wouldn't believe all the great stuff that I was doing as well.
paragraphs.cn: 听着，如果我走进告解室，对神父说，哦，我犯了这些罪，但你不会相信我同时也在做所有那些好事。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: It doesn't work. I never tried it because I'm afraid.
paragraphs.cn: 这行不通。我从未试过，因为我害怕。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You don't get fewer Hail Marys --
paragraphs.cn: 你不会少念万福玛利亚 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: No, no, no. In fact, I'd probably get a lot more. Look, I think, you know, I can't know what's in JD Vance's heart. I just think --
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，不。事实上，我可能会念更多。听着，我想，你知道，我无法知道J.D.万斯的内心。我只是觉得 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, he purports to tell us. I mean, he's on a book tour called for this book called "Communion" --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他声称要告诉我们。我的意思是，他正在为这本名为《圣餐》的书进行巡回宣传 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- which he is trying to show his heart.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 他试图借此展示他的内心。

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: Well, and I think that's admirable. And I know that, you know, as myself, as a work in progress, Christian and Catholic, that it is something you have to do every single day. But I find the sort of dismissal of the question about tone, of the words that the administration uses, and even J.D. Vance himself, I've written about this from the beginning of this administration --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为这值得赞赏。而且我知道，作为基督徒和天主教徒，我自己也在不断进步，这是你每天都必须做的事情。但我发现，对于政府使用的措辞、语气，甚至J.D.万斯本人，从一开始我就写过关于本届政府 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Warren
paragraphs.text: -- toward the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops, about the sort of immigration policy, that the words matter, the sort of things that we do every single day.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 对美国天主教主教会议的态度，关于移民政策，言辞很重要，我们每天做的事情也很重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.text: It's the words that matter. It's the policies that matter.
